# Mark MacMahon

> CTO at Luzia. Building ContextRocket. Three decades in software - Dublin, Washington DC, Brussels, Berlin, Madrid. Writing about AI, agents, architecture, and leadership.

This is a personal essay series. Posts are numbered and meant to be read in sequence, though each stands alone.

## Posts

- [01 Back to the Cursor](https://markmacmahon.com/posts/back-to-the-cursor/): From the Sinclair Spectrum to AI agents - some patterns do not change.
- [02 Uno. Dos. Tres. Catorce.](https://markmacmahon.com/posts/uno-dos-tres-catorce/): Hannover Quay, 2002-2004 - The Shape of Agents Before the World Was Ready.
- [03 Expand. Extract.](https://markmacmahon.com/posts/expand-extract/): How to build agent systems without drowning in possibility.

## Key Themes

- Personal agents outperform generic assistants because personality changes behaviour
- Privacy must be architectural, not bolted on
- Friction shapes what gets built - constraint is not the enemy, it is the design surface
- The bottleneck moved from typing to thinking
- Expand widely, extract decisively - operating discipline for building agent systems

## About the Author

Mark MacMahon is CTO at Luzia (consumer AI, 45M+ users) and solo founder of ContextRocket (AI platform for search visibility and brand positioning). Previously CTO at FLOYT, VP Engineering at iApps/Adevinta, and engineering leadership roles across Dublin, Washington DC, Brussels, Berlin, and Madrid. Nearly 30 years in software across identity systems, search, mobile, and AI.
